article.title: Sierra Raises $950 Million at Over $15 Billion Valuation, Pushing Bret Taylor's AI Customer-Service Startup Past $150 Million in ARR
article.summary: Tiger Global and GV led a $950 million round in Sierra, the AI customer-service startup co-founded by OpenAI chairman Bret Taylor, lifting its post-money valuation above $15 billion barely eight months after a $10 billion round.
article.body_markdown:
## Overview

Sierra, the AI customer-service startup co-founded by OpenAI chairman Bret Taylor, is raising a $950 million funding round led by Tiger Global and GV that pushes its post-money valuation above $15 billion, [TechCrunch reported](https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/04/sierra-raises-950m-as-the-race-to-own-enterprise-ai-gets-serious/) on Monday. The new round gives Sierra more than $1 billion in cash to deploy, and arrives roughly eight months after the company's last fundraise, a $350 million round at a $10 billion valuation led by Greenoaks Capital, [TechCrunch reported](https://techcrunch.com/2025/09/04/bret-taylors-sierra-raises-350m-at-a-10b-valuation/) at the time.

## What We Know

Sierra was founded in early 2024 by Taylor — formerly co-CEO of Salesforce, and now chairman of OpenAI — and Clay Bavor, who previously led virtual reality and Google Labs efforts at Google, [TechCrunch reported](https://techcrunch.com/2025/09/04/bret-taylors-sierra-raises-350m-at-a-10b-valuation/) when announcing the September 2025 round. The company's pre-Monday total funding stood at $635 million across earlier rounds, [per TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2025/09/04/bret-taylors-sierra-raises-350m-at-a-10b-valuation/).

The new round is led by Tiger Global and GV, [according to TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/04/sierra-raises-950m-as-the-race-to-own-enterprise-ai-gets-serious/). Sierra says the capital will go toward becoming the "global standard" for AI-powered customer experiences, [TechCrunch reported](https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/04/sierra-raises-950m-as-the-race-to-own-enterprise-ai-gets-serious/).

Sierra has been unusually public about its growth curve. The company [told TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/04/sierra-raises-950m-as-the-race-to-own-enterprise-ai-gets-serious/) it started with four design partners and now claims more than 40% of the Fortune 50 as customers, with platform agents handling billions of interactions across mortgage refinancing, insurance claims processing, returns management, and nonprofit fundraising. The startup first said it had hit $100 million in annual recurring revenue in late November, then announced $150 million in ARR in early February, [per TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/04/sierra-raises-950m-as-the-race-to-own-enterprise-ai-gets-serious/).

In April, Sierra launched Ghostwriter, an "agent as a service" tool that takes a natural-language description of a task and autonomously builds and deploys a specialized agent to handle it, [TechCrunch reported](https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/04/sierra-raises-950m-as-the-race-to-own-enterprise-ai-gets-serious/). At the HumanX conference in San Francisco last month, Taylor argued that most enterprise software is barely used — employees log into Workday only when they onboard and again at open enrollment, he said — and that the future Sierra is betting on is one where workers never navigate complex systems at all, [according to TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/04/sierra-raises-950m-as-the-race-to-own-enterprise-ai-gets-serious/). In a [separate TechCrunch interview](https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/09/sierras-bret-taylor-says-the-era-of-clicking-buttons-is-over/) in April, Taylor framed the same thesis more bluntly: "Most companies don't want to make software. They want solutions to their problems."

## Customer Adoption Signals

The announcement landed alongside customer-side data points suggesting agentic deployments are scaling fast. At one of TechCrunch's StrictlyVC events last week, Uber CTO Praveen Neppalli Naga said Uber "blew through our [AI] budget" soon after opening the door to agentic AI tools late last year, [TechCrunch reported](https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/04/sierra-raises-950m-as-the-race-to-own-enterprise-ai-gets-serious/). Across roughly 8,000 engineers and technical workers, about 10% of all code at Uber is now generated autonomously, Neppalli Naga said, adding that "10% at our scale is huge," [per TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/04/sierra-raises-950m-as-the-race-to-own-enterprise-ai-gets-serious/). One Uber team built a hotel-booking integration using only agentic workflows, finishing in six months work that would normally take a year, [according to the same TechCrunch report](https://techcrunch.com/2026/05/04/sierra-raises-950m-as-the-race-to-own-enterprise-ai-gets-serious/).

## What We Don't Know

TechCrunch's report did not name Sierra customers individually beyond the Fortune-50 footprint figure, and did not disclose the round's full investor syndicate beyond the lead pair of Tiger Global and GV. The company's gross margins, retention figures, and the path from $150 million ARR to a justification for a $15 billion-plus valuation are not laid out in the available reporting. Nor is there public information on when, or whether, Sierra intends to file for an IPO.

The pace of the valuation step-up — from $10 billion in early September to above $15 billion roughly eight months later — also leaves open the question of how durable enterprise demand for AI customer-service agents will be once the current wave of pilot-budget spending settles into recurring contracts. Taylor's pitch, as captured in [TechCrunch's April interview](https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/09/sierras-bret-taylor-says-the-era-of-clicking-buttons-is-over/), is that the shift away from click-driven enterprise software is structural rather than cyclical; the next several quarters of ARR disclosure will start to test that claim against the new valuation.
